if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  get_p_table(
    pwc_biomarkers_overall,
    "Overall Biomarker Pairwise Comparisons"
  )
  get_p_table(
    pwc_confounders_overall,
    "Overall Confounder Pairwise Comparisons"
  )
}

7.2.4 Add p-values to summary dataframe

import_p_values <- function(summary_data, pwc_data, per_age_group = FALSE) {
  grouping_variables <- if (per_age_group) c("predictors", "age_group") else "predictors"

  updated_summary_data <- summary_data %>%
    left_join(
      pwc_data %>%
        select(
          all_of(grouping_variables),
          p_adj,
          signif
        ),
      by = c(grouping_variables)
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      p_adj = as.character(p_adj),
      p_adj = case_when(
        signif == "ns" ~ "ns",
        is.na(p_adj) ~ "ns",
        TRUE ~ p_adj
      ),
      signif = case_when(
        is.na(signif) ~ "ns",
        TRUE ~ signif
      )
    )

  updated_summary_data
}

df_summary_bioage <- df_summary_bioage %>%
  import_p_values(
    pwc_bioage,
    per_age_group = TRUE
  )

df_summary_bioage_overall <- df_summary_bioage_overall %>%
  import_p_values(
    pwc_bioage_overall
  )

if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  df_summary_biomarkers <- df_summary_biomarkers %>%
    import_p_values(
      pwc_biomarkers,
      per_age_group = TRUE
    )

  df_summary_biomarkers_overall <- df_summary_biomarkers_overall %>%
    import_p_values(
      pwc_biomarkers_overall
    )

  df_summary_confounders <- df_summary_confounders %>%
    import_p_values(
      pwc_confounders,
      per_age_group = TRUE
    )
  df_summary_confounders_overall <- df_summary_confounders_overall %>%
    import_p_values(
      pwc_confounders_overall
    )
}

It doesn’t matter whether we use holm or hommel, so perhaps we should use hommel.

7.2.5 Add raw Ns to dataframe

add_n_age_groups <- function(summary_data, data, per_age_group = FALSE) {
  if (per_age_group) {
    summary_data <- summary_data %>%
      rename("n_folds_repeats" = n) %>%
      left_join(
        data %>%
          select(age_group) %>%
          group_by(age_group) %>%
          summarize(n = n()),
        by = "age_group"
      )
  } else {
    summary_data <- summary_data %>%
      rename("n_folds_repeats" = n) %>%
      mutate(n = nrow(data))
  }

  summary_data
}

df_summary_bioage <- df_summary_bioage %>%
  add_n_age_groups(
    data = df,
    per_age_group = TRUE
  )
df_summary_bioage_overall <- df_summary_bioage_overall %>%
  add_n_age_groups(
    data = df
  )

if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  df_summary_biomarkers <- df_summary_biomarkers %>%
    add_n_age_groups(
      data = df,
      per_age_group = TRUE
    )
  df_summary_biomarkers_overall <- df_summary_biomarkers_overall %>%
    add_n_age_groups(
      data = df
    )

  df_summary_confounders <- df_summary_confounders %>%
    add_n_age_groups(
      data = df,
      per_age_group = TRUE
    )
  df_summary_confounders_overall <- df_summary_confounders_overall %>%
    add_n_age_groups(
      data = df
    )
}

7.3 Confounder model comparison

if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  anova(
    output_confounders$models$full,
    output_confounders$models$full_with_phenoage
  )
  anova(
    output_confounders$models$full,
    output_confounders$models$no_gender
  )
  anova(
    output_confounders$models$full,
    output_confounders$models$no_smoking
  )
  anova(
    output_confounders$models$full,
    output_confounders$models$no_race
  )
  anova(
    output_confounders$models$full,
    output_confounders$models$no_education
  )
  anova(
    output_confounders$models$full,
    output_confounders$models$`no_gender, race`
  )
}

7.4 Assess Rate of Aging

7.4.1 Compare Linear vs Quadratic vs Smooth Age Fits

We do a partial F-test to compare a full model with a constrained model, to see if the added term significantly improves fit.

anova(
  output_age_rate$models$linear,
  output_age_rate$models$quadratic
)
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: FI_low ~ age
## Model 2: FI_low ~ age + I(age^2)
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance      F    Pr(>F)    
## 1      2358     132599                                 
## 2      2357     129178  1   3420.5 62.411 4.238e-15 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(
  output_age_rate$models$quadratic,
  output_age_rate$models$smooth
)
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: FI_low ~ age + I(age^2)
## Model 2: FI_low ~ age + I(age^2) + s(age, k = 10)
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev      Df Deviance      F Pr(>F)
## 1    2357.0     129178                               
## 2    2356.6     129165 0.37137   13.539 0.6652 0.2783
c(
  linear = sqrt(
    mean(
      residuals(output_age_rate$models$linear, type = "response")^2
    )
  ),
  quadratic = sqrt(
    mean(
      residuals(output_age_rate$models$quadratic, type = "response")^2
    )
  ),
  smooth = sqrt(
    mean(
      residuals(output_age_rate$models$smooth, type = "response")^2
    )
  )
) %>%
  get_p_table(title = "RMSE (In-Sample)")
## <div style="border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 0px; overflow-y: scroll; height:400px; overflow-x: scroll; width:100%; "><table class="table table-striped table-hover" style="font-size: 12px; color: black; width: auto !important; ">
## <caption style="font-size: initial !important;">RMSE (In-Sample)</caption>
##  <thead>
##   <tr>
##    <th style="text-align:left;position: sticky; top:0; background-color: #FFFFFF;position: sticky; top:0; background-color: #FFFFFF;">   </th>
##    <th style="text-align:right;position: sticky; top:0; background-color: #FFFFFF;position: sticky; top:0; background-color: #FFFFFF;"> x </th>
##   </tr>
##  </thead>
## <tbody>
##   <tr>
##    <td style="text-align:left;"> linear </td>
##    <td style="text-align:right;"> 7.495724 </td>
##   </tr>
##   <tr>
##    <td style="text-align:left;"> quadratic </td>
##    <td style="text-align:right;"> 7.398413 </td>
##   </tr>
##   <tr>
##    <td style="text-align:left;"> smooth </td>
##    <td style="text-align:right;"> 7.398025 </td>
##   </tr>
## </tbody>
## </table></div>

We see that the quadratic curve fits better than the linear one, so the rate of aging is not linear, and the smooth curve fits better than the quadratic one, so there are deviations from a quadratic pattern

7.4.2 Assess Where Rate of Aging Differs

By taking the derivative of the full model (with smooth) and subtracting the derivative of the nested quadratic model, we can test against zero to test deviations from a quadratic pattern.

When making a smooth (or linear) model, we fit a line to noisy data as best we can, but other lines with different coefficients might fit almost as well and be better for some data points then for others. Therefore, there is an uncertainty. We can say that, for example, the best-guess slope is 5 (5 increase fletcher index per year). But because the data is noisy, assuming a normal distribution, we know that the true slope that would actually fit the sampling population may be something different within a certain range of uncertainty (say, 2), which we can express as a standard error. When figuring out the derivative, we inherent this uncertainty. This standard error is what we can use to assess the likelihood of the null-hypothesis:

H0: The rate of aging is quadratic, so the GAM derivative minus the quadratic coefficients equals 0. H1: The rate of aging is non-quadratic, so the GAM derivative minus the quadratic coefficients is not 0.

With a p-value of 0.05, we can say that if we randomly sample 100 times from the population and the rate of aging IS linear, then 5 times we’ll see a significant non-zero smooth term.

Depending on the variation/noise in the data, the smooth term derivative needs to be larger or smaller compared to 0 to get a p-value of 0.05. If the variation in the data is higher, the standard error is higher, and the derivative of the smooth term must be higher to get a p-value of 0.05 or lower.

Of course, we want to know at which age ranges we have a non-zero slope, so we can either test for each 10 year age group or test for each individual age integer with correct pairwise comparison corrections.

A two-tailed p-value can be calculated when we know a z-score, which is calculated as sample mean - hypothesized mean divided by the standard error. The hypothesized mean is 0. For a two-tailed t-test, we then take 2 * pnorm(abs(z_score), lower.tail = FALSE). If we’d want to only know the left tail, we’d use lower.tail = TRUE.

Because the t-tests are dependent and are likely to be either higher or lower when one is higher (because ages are adjacent and the aging rate can be either higher or lower), we should use Holm’s correction.

To identify significant boundaries, check which pairwise comparisons are significant, then check if the previous row was significant, then whenever a row is significant but the previous one was, change the value by 1 (using cumsum), or if the previous row was not significant but the current one is, also change by 1 to identify stretches. Then take the first and last values of the stretches to identify boundaries. Only keep the significant stretches.

pwc_age_rate <- output_age_rate$predictions$derivative_smooth %>%
  mutate(
    z_score = derivative_adj / SE,
    p = 2 * pnorm(abs(z_score), lower.tail = FALSE),
    p_adj = p.adjust(p, method = "holm"),
    signif = case_when(
      p_adj <= 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_adj <= 0.01 ~ "**",
      p_adj <= 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age,
    derivative,
    ci_lower,
    ci_upper,
    derivative_adj,
    ci_lower_adj,
    ci_upper_adj,
    p,
    p_adj,
    signif
  )

pwc_age_rate_boundaries <- pwc_age_rate %>%
  mutate(
    significant = (p_adj < 0.05),
    run = cumsum(
      significant != lag(
        significant,
        default = first(significant)
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(run, significant) %>%
  summarise(
    x_min = first(age),
    x_max = last(age),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    `Significantly non-linear age ranges` = paste0(x_min, "–", x_max)
  ) %>%
  filter(significant)

pwc_age_rate_boundaries %>%
  select(`Significantly non-linear age ranges`) %>%
  get_p_table(title = "Boundaries")
## <div style="border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 0px; overflow-y: scroll; height:400px; overflow-x: scroll; width:100%; "><table class="table table-striped table-hover" style="font-size: 12px; color: black; width: auto !important; ">
## <caption style="font-size: initial !important;">Boundaries</caption>
##  <thead>
##   <tr>
##    <th style="text-align:left;position: sticky; top:0; background-color: #FFFFFF;position: sticky; top:0; background-color: #FFFFFF;"> Significantly non-linear age ranges </th>
##   </tr>
##  </thead>
## <tbody>
##   <tr>
## 
##   </tr>
## </tbody>
## </table></div>

8 Visualization

8.1 Tables

8.1.0.1 Descriptives table

8.1.0.2 Format table

Round R² and delta RMSE, rename variables, set p values to bold and to ” < 0.001” when very small.

format_table <- function(summary_data, per_age_group = FALSE) {
  table <- summary_data %>%
    mutate(
      r2 = round(r2, 3),
      delta_rmse = round(delta_rmse, 2)
    ) %>%
    rename(
      "Predictors" = predictors,
      "∆RMSE (vs age, dB HL)" = delta_rmse,
      "R²" = r2,
      "p (vs age)" = p_adj
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      `p (vs age)` = case_when(
        signif == "***" ~
          cell_spec("< 0.001", "html", bold = TRUE),
        suppressWarnings(
          !is.na(
            as.numeric(`p (vs age)`)
          ) &
            as.numeric(`p (vs age)`) < 0.05
        ) &
          `p (vs age)` != "< 0.001" ~
          cell_spec(
            suppressWarnings(
              round(
                as.numeric(`p (vs age)`),
                3
              )
            ),
            "html",
            bold = TRUE
          ),
        TRUE ~ `p (vs age)`
      ),
      `p (vs age)` = as.character(`p (vs age)`),
      `p (vs age)` = case_when(Predictors == "age" ~ "", TRUE ~ `p (vs age)`),
      `∆RMSE (vs age, dB HL)` = as.character(`∆RMSE (vs age, dB HL)`),
      `∆RMSE (vs age, dB HL)` = case_when(
        Predictors == "age" ~ "",
        TRUE ~ `∆RMSE (vs age, dB HL)`
      )
    )

  if (isTRUE(per_age_group)) {
    table <- table %>%
      rename("Age" = age_group) %>%
      select(Predictors, Age, n, `R²`, `∆RMSE (vs age, dB HL)`, `p (vs age)`)
  } else {
    table <- table %>%
      select(Predictors, n, `R²`, `∆RMSE (vs age, dB HL)`, `p (vs age)`)
  }

  table <- table %>%
    rename(
      "p (vs full model)" = `p (vs age)`,
      "∆RMSE (vs full model, dB HL)" = `∆RMSE (vs age, dB HL)`,
    )

  table
}

8.1.0.3 Order, Filter, and Rename Tables

rename_bioage_table <- function(bioage_table) {
  bioage_table <- bioage_table %>%
    mutate(
      Predictors = case_when(
        Predictors == "age" ~ "Age",
        Predictors == "age, hd_log" ~ "Age + HD",
        Predictors == "age, kdm" ~ "Age + KDM",
        Predictors == "age, phenoage" ~ "Age + PhenoAge",
        isTRUE(params$telolength) & Predictors == "age, telolength" ~ "Age + Telolength",
        TRUE ~ Predictors
      ),
      Predictors = factor(
        Predictors,
        levels = if (isTRUE(params$telolength)) {
          c("Age", "Age + HD", "Age + KDM", "Age + PhenoAge", "Age + Telolength")
        } else {
          c("Age", "Age + HD", "Age + KDM", "Age + PhenoAge")
        }
      )
    ) %>%
    arrange(Predictors)

  bioage_table
}

rename_biomarker_table <- function(biomarker_table) {
  biomarker_table <- biomarker_table %>%
    filter(!
    (
      Predictors %in% c(
        "age",
        "age, phenoage",
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL",
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL, phenoage"
      )
    )) %>%
    mutate(
      Predictors = case_when(
        Predictors == "age, rdw" ~ "Age + RDW",
        Predictors == "age, alp" ~ "Age + ALP",
        Predictors == "age, crp" ~ "Age + CRP",
        Predictors == "age, totchol" ~ "Age + TC",
        Predictors == "age, albumin_gL" ~ "Age + ALB",
        Predictors == "age, hba1c" ~ "Age + HbA1c",
        Predictors == "age, glucose" ~ "Age + Glc",
        Predictors == "age, sbp" ~ "Age + SBP",
        Predictors == "age, bun" ~ "Age + BUN",
        Predictors == "age, mcv" ~ "Age + MCV",
        Predictors == "age, wbc" ~ "Age + WBC",
        Predictors == "age, lymph" ~ "Age + Lymphs",
        Predictors == "age, uap" ~ "Age + UA",
        Predictors == "age, creat" ~ "Age + Creat"
      ),
      Predictors = factor(Predictors, levels = c(
        "Age + SBP",
        "Age + BUN",
        "Age + MCV",
        "Age + Lymphs",
        "Age + RDW",
        "Age + TC",
        "Age + Creat",
        "Age + UA",
        "Age + Glc",
        "Age + WBC",
        "Age + ALP",
        "Age + ALB",
        "Age + HbA1c",
        "Age + CRP"
      ))
    ) %>%
    arrange(Predictors)

  biomarker_table
}

rename_confounder_table <- function(confounders_table) {
  confounders_table <- confounders_table %>%
    filter(!(
      Predictors %in% c("full")
    )) %>%
    mutate(
      Predictors = case_when(
        Predictors == "full_with_phenoage" ~ "Phenoage",
        Predictors == "no_gender" ~ "Gender",
        Predictors == "no_smoking" ~ "Smoking",
        Predictors == "no_race" ~ "Race",
        Predictors == "no_education" ~ "Education",
        Predictors == "no_gender, race" ~ "Gender * Race"
      ),
      Predictors = factor(Predictors, levels = c(
        "Phenoage",
        "Gender",
        "Smoking",
        "Race",
        "Education",
        "Gender * Race"
      ))
    ) %>%
    arrange(Predictors)

  confounders_table
}

8.1.0.4 Run table formatting pipeline

table_bioage <- df_summary_bioage %>%
  format_table(
    per_age_group = TRUE
  ) %>%
  rename_bioage_table()

table_bioage_overall <- df_summary_bioage_overall %>%
  format_table() %>%
  rename_bioage_table()

if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  table_biomarkers <- df_summary_biomarkers %>%
    format_table(
      per_age_group = TRUE
    ) %>%
    rename_biomarker_table()

  table_biomarkers_overall <- df_summary_biomarkers_overall %>%
    format_table() %>%
    rename_biomarker_table()

  table_confounders <- df_summary_confounders %>%
    format_table(
      per_age_group = TRUE
    ) %>%
    rename_confounder_table()

  table_confounders_overall <- df_summary_confounders_overall %>%
    format_table() %>%
    rename_confounder_table()
}

8.1.0.5 Render table

render_and_save_table <- function(table, path) {
  html_table <- table %>%
    kable(
      format = "html",
      align = c("l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r"),
      escape = FALSE
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
      full_width = FALSE,
      position = "center"
    ) %>%
    collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")

  if (isTRUE(params$save_figures)) {
    save_kable(html_table, path)
  }

  html_table
}

render_and_save_table(
  table = table_bioage,
  path = here("Output", "Figures", params$hearing_var, "table_bioage.html")
)
Predictors Age n R² ∆RMSE (vs full model, dB HL) p (vs full model)
Age 20-30 478 0.389
30-40 501 0.198
40-50 516 0.083
50-60 403 0.116
60-70 462 0.335
Age + HD 20-30 478 0.389 0 ns
30-40 501 0.208 0.03 ns
40-50 516 0.105 0.08 ns
50-60 403 0.126 0.05 ns
60-70 462 0.336 0 ns
Age + KDM 20-30 478 0.387 -0.01 ns
30-40 501 0.223 0.09 ns
40-50 516 0.098 0.06 ns
50-60 403 0.136 0.09 ns
60-70 462 0.336 0.01 ns
Age + PhenoAge 20-30 478 0.389 0 ns
30-40 501 0.211 0.05 ns
40-50 516 0.094 0.04 ns
50-60 403 0.127 0.05 ns
60-70 462 0.336 0 ns
Age + Telolength 20-30 478 0.390 0 ns
30-40 501 0.199 0 ns
40-50 516 0.083 0 ns
50-60 403 0.114 -0.01 ns
60-70 462 0.334 -0.01 ns 
render_and_save_table(
  table = table_bioage_overall,
  path = here("Output", "Figures", params$hearing_var, "table_bioage_overall.html")
)
Predictors n R² ∆RMSE (vs full model, dB HL) p (vs full model)
Age 2360 0.250
Age + HD 2360 0.257 0.03 ns
Age + KDM 2360 0.259 0.04 ns
Age + PhenoAge 2360 0.256 0.03 ns
Age + Telolength 2360 0.250 0 ns 
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  render_and_save_table(
    table = table_biomarkers,
    path = here("Output", "Figures", params$hearing_var, "table_biomarkers.html")
  ) %>%
    scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "400px")

  render_and_save_table(
    table = table_biomarkers_overall,
    path = here("Output", "Figures", params$hearing_var, "table_biomarkers_overall.html")
  )

  render_and_save_table(
    table = table_confounders,
    path = here("Output", "Figures", params$hearing_var, "table_confounders.html")
  ) %>%
    scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "400px")

  render_and_save_table(
    table = table_confounders_overall,
    path = here("Output", "Figures", params$hearing_var, "table_confounders_overall.html")
  )
}

8.1.1 Confounders table

8.1.1.1 Build table and recode values

Recode has a namespace conflict and is called explicitly from dplyr.

build_confounder_table <- function(data, y = params$hearing_var) {
  variables <- c("Gender", "Smoking", "Race", "Education")

  data <- data %>%
    mutate(
      gender = dplyr::recode(
        gender,
        "1" = "Male",
        "2" = "Female"
      ),
      smoking = dplyr::recode(
        smoking,
        "1" = "Smoker",
        "2" = "Non-smoker"
      ),
      race = dplyr::recode(
        race,
        "1" = "Hispanic",
        "3" = "White",
        "4" = "Black",
        "5" = "Other"
      ),
      education = dplyr::recode(
        education,
        "1" = "< 9th grade",
        "2" = "9-11th grade",
        "3" = "High school",
        "4" = "Some college or AA degree",
        "5" = "College graduate or higher"
      )
    ) %>%
    rename(
      "Smoking" = smoking,
      "Gender" = gender,
      "Race" = race,
      "Education" = education
    )

  bind_rows(
    lapply(variables, function(variable) {
      data %>%
        mutate(level = .data[[variable]]) %>%
        group_by(age_group, level) %>%
        summarise(
          mean = mean(.data[[y]], na.rm = TRUE),
          sd = sd(.data[[y]], na.rm = TRUE),
          .groups = "drop"
        ) %>%
        mutate(
          variable = factor(
            variable,
            levels = c(
              "Gender",
              "Smoking",
              "Race",
              "Education"
            )
          ),
          .before = 1
        )
    })
  )
}

8.1.1.2 Create confounder table and format confounder table

We recode variables, combine means and SDs, pivot table, and format it

if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  table_confounders_descriptive <- df %>%
    build_confounder_table() %>%
    mutate(stat = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", mean, sd)) %>%
    select(variable, level, age_group, stat) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = age_group, values_from = stat) %>%
    group_by(variable) %>%
    mutate(variable = if_else(row_number() == 1L, variable, "")) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    rename(" " = variable, "  " = level)
}

8.1.1.3 Save table

if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  render_and_save_table(
    table_confounders_descriptive,
    path = here(
      "Output",
      "Figures",
      params$hearing_var,
      "table_confounders_descriptives.html"
    )
  )
}

8.2 Figures

8.2.1 Set plot config

8.2.1.1 Set plot styling variables

We store a separate configuration depending on output type of figure for easy switching between output types. Only the currently selected output type variables in params + global variables for plotting we may need later is returned. These variables are used to construct ggplot protoobjects.

plot_config <- function(variable = NULL, global_params = params) {
  p_cfg_vars <- list(
    global = list(
      axis_unit_color = "#4D4D4D" # used later when constructing axes from HTML
    ),
    poster = list(),
    paper = list(
      figure_dir = here(
        "Output",
        "Figures",
        if (isTRUE(global_params$telolength)) {
          paste0(global_params$hearing_var, "_telolength")
        } else {
          global_params$hearing_var
        }
      ),
      text_size_primary = 8,
      text_size_secondary = 9,
      signif_size = 14
    )
  )

  p_cfg_vars_comb <- c(p_cfg_vars$global, p_cfg_vars[[global_params$plot_output_type]])
  
  if (!is.null(variable)) p_cfg_vars_comb[[variable]] else p_cfg_vars_comb
}

8.2.1.2 Store general plot theme

We first store the theme preset (theme_few), then theme overrides, then other theme items like guides. Upon initial assignment of the theme settings, we read directly from plot_config(). The plot output type variables set in the previous chunk are also appended, so that they can be accessed either with p_cfg or with plot_config(). All items in the plot_theme list must be ggplot proto objects. Repeated plot_config() calls are used to avoid creating another unnecessary variable. Setting the theme with repeated calls takes < 200ms, so clarity of intent with only declaring one list of p_cfg is preferred

plot_theme <- function(global_params = params) {
  config <- if (!is.null(global_params)) plot_config(global_params = global_params)

  list(
    theme_few(),
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_markdown("sans", size = config$text_size_primary),
      axis.text.y = element_markdown("sans", size = config$text_size_primary),
      strip.text.x = element_markdown(
        "sans",
        size = config$text_size_secondary,
        lineheight = 1.1
      ),
      strip.text.y = element_markdown(
        "sans",
        size = config$text_size_secondary,
        lineheight = 1.1
      ),
      axis.title.x = element_markdown(
        "sans",
        size = config$text_size_secondary,
        lineheight = 1.1
      ),
      axis.title.y = element_markdown(
        "sans",
        size = config$text_size_secondary,
        lineheight = 1.1
      ),
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      legend.key = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
      legend.position = "inside",
      legend.justification.inside = c(0, 1),
      legend.key.spacing.y = unit(1, "pt"),
      panel.border = element_part_rect(
        colour = "grey30",
        side = "lb",
        linewidth = 0.5
      ),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 0, 1, unit = "pt")
    ),
    guides(
      colour = guide_legend(
        override.aes = list(size = 1.25),
        keywidth = unit(0.3, "cm"),
        keyheight = unit(0.3, "cm"),
        label.theme = element_markdown(
          "sans",
          size = config$text_size_secondary,
          margin = margin(l = 3, t = 1.5, unit = "pt")
        )
      )
    )
  )
}

8.2.1.3 Set axis scales

Available scales can be inspected with str(get_scales(), max.level = 2).

axes <- function(x = NULL, y = NULL, global_params = params) {
  config <- if (!is.null(global_params)) plot_config(global_params = global_params)

  if (!is.null(x)) {
    x <- switch(x,
      age = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "x",
        type = "continuous",
        breaks = seq(20, 80, 10),
        limits = c(20, 82),
        name = "Age",
        unit = "Years"
      ),
      age_group = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "x",
        type = "discrete",
        limits = c(1.4, 5.6),
        name = "Age",
        unit = "Years"
      ),
      age_accel = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "x",
        type = "continuous",
        limits = c(-20, 20),
        name = "Age Acceleration",
        unit = "PhenoAge – Age (Years)"
      )
    )
  }

  if (!is.null(y)) {
    y <- switch(y,
      age_group = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "y",
        type = "discrete",
        limits = c(1, 7),
        name = "Age",
        unit = "Years"
      ),
      hl = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "y",
        type = "continuous",
        breaks = seq(0, 90, 30),
        limits = c(-10, 100),
        name = paste0(if (params$hearing_var == "FI_high") "High" else "Low", " Fletcher Index"),
        unit = "dB HL"
      ),
      hl_rate = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "y",
        type = "continuous",
        breaks = seq(0, 2, 1),
        limits = c(-0.5, 2.6),
        name = "Rate of HL",
        unit = "dB HL/Year"
      ),
      hl_diff = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "y",
        type = "continuous",
        breaks = seq(0, 2, 1),
        limits = c(-0.5, 2.6),
        name = "Deviation Rate of HL",
        unit = "dB HL/Year"
      ),
      dmse_bioage = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "y",
        type = "continuous",
        breaks = seq(0, 8, 2),
        limits = c(-1, 8.5),
        name = "Additional Predictive Capacity",
        unit = "Δ MSE (dB HL)<sup>2</sup>"
      ),
      drmse_bioage = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "y",
        type = "continuous",
        breaks = seq(0, 0.25, 0.1),
        limits = c(-0.05, 0.25),
        name = "Additional Predictive Capacity",
        unit = "Δ RMSE (dB HL)"
      ),
      dmse_biomark = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "y",
        type = "continuous",
        breaks = seq(0, 2, 1),
        limits = c(-0.25, 1.5),
        name = "Additional Predictive Capacity",
        unit = "Δ MSE (dB HL)<sup>2</sup>"
      ),
      dmse_conf = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "y",
        type = "continuous",
        breaks = seq(0, 40, 5),
        limits = c(-1, 40),
        name = "Additional Predictive Capacity",
        unit = "Δ MSE (dB HL)<sup>2</sup>"
      ),
      dmse_best_biomark = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "y",
        type = "continuous",
        breaks = c(0, 2),
        limits = c(-1.5, 4),
        name = "Additional Predictive Capacity",
        unit = "Δ MSE (dB HL)<sup>2</sup>"
      ),
      add_pred_conf = define_axis(
        config = config,
        axis = "y",
        type = "continuous",
        breaks = seq(0, 14, 2),
        limits = c(-1, 15),
        name = "Additional Predictive Capacity",
        unit = "% Change MSE"
      )
    )
  }

  build_axes(x = x, y = y)
}

8.2.1.4 Set colors

Available colors can be inspected with str(color_scales(), max.level = 2).

color_scales <- function(color_list_name) {
  switch(color_list_name,
    bioage = build_color_scale(
      colors = c(
        "age" = "#000000",
        "age, hd_log" = "#256DAB",
        "age, kdm" = "#059748",
        "age, phenoage" = "#CB2027",
        "age, telolength" = if (isTRUE(params$telolength)) "#7B3A96" else NULL
      ),
      fill_colors = c(
        "age" = "#8C8C8C",
        "age, hd_log" = "#88BDE6",
        "age, kdm" = "#90CD97",
        "age, phenoage" = "#F07E6E",
        "age, telolength" = if (isTRUE(params$telolength)) "#B276B2" else NULL
      ),
      labels = c(
        "age" = "Age",
        "age, hd_log" = "Age + HD",
        "age, kdm" = "Age + KDM",
        "age, phenoage" = "Age + PhenoAge",
        "age, telolength" = if (isTRUE(params$telolength)) "Age + Telolength" else NULL
      )
    ),
    confounders = build_color_scale(
      colors = c(
        "full" = "#000000",
        "no_gender" = "#059748",
        "no_smoking" = "#9D722A",
        "no_race" = "#E5126F",
        "no_education" = "#DF5C24",
        "no_gender, race" = "#7B3A96",
        "full_with_phenoage" = "#256DAB"
      ),
      fill_colors = c(
        "full" = "#8C8C8C",
        "no_gender" = "#90CD97",
        "no_smoking" = "#BFA554",
        "no_race" = "#F6AAC9",
        "no_education" = "#FBB258",
        "no_gender, race" = "#BC99C7",
        "full_with_phenoage" = "#88BDE6"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "full" = "Full",
        "no_gender" = "Gender",
        "no_smoking" = "Smoking",
        "no_race" = "Race",
        "no_education" = "Education",
        "no_gender, race" = "Gender*Race",
        "full_with_phenoage" = "Phenoage"
      )
    ),
    phenoage = build_color_scale(
      colors = c(
        "age" = "#000000",
        "age, phenoage" = "#CB2027"
      ),
      fill_colors = c(
        "age" = "#8C8C8C",
        "age, phenoage" = "#F07E6E"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "age" = "Age",
        "age, phenoage" = "Age + PhenoAge"
      )
    ),
    gender = build_color_scale(
      colors = c(
        "1" = "#2986cc",
        "2" = "#c90076"
      ),
      fill_colors = c(
        "1" = "#6fa8dc",
        "2" = "#d5a6bd"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "1" = "Male",
        "2" = "Female"
      )
    ),
    race = build_color_scale(
      colors = c(
        "1" = "#E5126F",
        "3" = "#DF5C24",
        "4" = "#7B3A96",
        "5" = "#256DAB"
      ),
      fill_colors = c(
        "1" = "#F6AAC9",
        "3" = "#FBB258",
        "4" = "#BC99C7",
        "5" = "#88BDE6"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "1" = "Hispanic",
        "3" = "White",
        "4" = "Black",
        "5" = "Other"
      )
    ),
    biomarker_diff = build_color_scale(
      colors = c(
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL, phenoage" = "#CB2027",
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL" = "#059748",
        "age, phenoage" = "grey65"
      ),
      fill_colors = c(
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL, phenoage" = "#F07E6E",
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL" = "#90CD97",
        "age, phenoage" = "grey80"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL, phenoage" = "Age + CRP + HbA1c + ALB + PhenoAge",
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL" = "Age + CRP + HbA1c + ALB",
        "age, phenoage" = "Age + PhenoAge"
      ),
      breaks = c(
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL, phenoage",
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL",
        "age, phenoage"
      )
    ),
    best_biomarkers = build_color_scale(
      colors = c(
        "age, crp" = "#256DAB",
        "age, hba1c" = "#E5126F",
        "age, albumin_gL" = "#DF5C24"
      ),
      fill_colors = c(
        "age, crp" = "#88BDE6",
        "age, hba1c" = "#F6AAC9",
        "age, albumin_gL" = "#FBB258"
      )
    )
  )
}

8.2.1.5 Set geom style

Note that using update_geom_defaults() is global and requires reset_geom_defaults() to be reset. update_geom_defaults() is only set once when switching to a different plot type and subsequent plots with the same styling will not have repeated update_geom_defaults() calls to avoid overhead and clutter. Plots must therefore be run in order.

set_geom_theme <- function(geom_theme_name, global_params = params) {
  config <- if (!is.null(global_params)) plot_config(global_params = global_params)

  switch(geom_theme_name,
    ribbon = list(
      alpha = 0.15
    ),
    highlight_rect = list(
      alpha = 0.125,
      fill = "#ff0000"
    ),
    hline = list(
      linetype = "22",
      color = "grey20",
      linewidth = 0.2
    ),
    age_rate_point = list(
      alpha = 0.1,
      size = 0.5,
      shape = 1,
      stroke = 0.25,
      color = "grey30"
    ),
    age_rate_line = list(
      linewidth = 0.6,
      alpha = 1
    ),
    mse_point = list(
      size = 1.25,
      alpha = 1,
      shape = 16,
      stroke = 0.5
    ),
    mse_line = list(
      linewidth = 0.7,
      alpha = 1
    ),
    biomarker_linerange = list(
      linewidth = 0.75
    ),
    biomarker_point = list(
      size = 2.25,
      alpha = 1,
      shape = 16,
      stroke = 0.5
    )
  )
}

update_geom_defaults("ribbon", set_geom_theme("ribbon"))
update_geom_defaults("rect", set_geom_theme("highlight_rect"))
update_geom_defaults("hline", set_geom_theme("hline"))

8.2.2 Rate of aging panel

8.2.2.1 Main panel (paper)

update_geom_defaults("point", set_geom_theme("age_rate_point"))
update_geom_defaults("line", set_geom_theme("age_rate_line"))

p0 <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = df,
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
    )
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$linear,
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = estimate
    )
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$linear,
    aes(
      x = age,
      ymin = ci_lower,
      ymax = ci_upper
    )
  ) +
  axes(
    x = "age",
    y = "hl"
  ) +
  plot_theme() +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(1, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt"),
    axis.title.x = element_blank()
  )

p1 <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = df,
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
    )
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$quadratic,
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = estimate
    )
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$quadratic,
    aes(
      x = age,
      ymin = ci_lower,
      ymax = ci_upper
    )
  ) +
  axes(
    x = "age",
    y = "hl"
  ) +
  plot_theme() +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(1, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt"),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank()
  )

p2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_rect(
    data = pwc_age_rate_boundaries,
    aes(
      xmin = x_min,
      xmax = x_max,
      ymin = -Inf,
      ymax = Inf
    )
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = df,
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
    )
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$smooth,
    aes(
      x = age,
      ymin = ci_lower + coef(output_age_rate$models$smooth)["(Intercept)"],
      ymax = ci_upper + coef(output_age_rate$models$smooth)["(Intercept)"]
    )
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$smooth,
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = estimate + coef(output_age_rate$models$smooth)["(Intercept)"]
    )
  ) +
  axes(
    x = "age",
    y = "hl"
  ) +
  plot_theme() +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 0, 0, unit = "pt"),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank()
  )

p3 <- ggplot() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_line(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$linear_derivative,
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = derivative
    )
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$linear_derivative,
    aes(
      x = age,
      ymin = ci_lower,
      ymax = ci_upper
    )
  ) +
  axes(
    x = "age",
    y = "hl_rate"
  ) +
  plot_theme() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt"))

p4 <- ggplot() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_line(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$quadratic_derivative,
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = derivative
    )
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$quadratic_derivative,
    aes(
      x = age,
      ymin = ci_lower,
      ymax = ci_upper
    )
  ) +
  axes(
    x = "age",
    y = "hl_rate"
  ) +
  plot_theme() +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt"),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  )

p5 <- ggplot() +
  geom_rect(
    data = pwc_age_rate_boundaries,
    aes(
      xmin = x_min,
      xmax = x_max,
      ymin = -Inf,
      ymax = Inf
    )
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$derivative_smooth,
    aes(
      x = age,
      ymin = ci_lower,
      ymax = ci_upper
    )
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$derivative_smooth,
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = derivative
    )
  ) +
  axes(
    x = "age",
    y = "hl_rate"
  ) +
  plot_theme() +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(0, 1, 0, 0, unit = "pt"),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  )

p6 <- ggplot() +
  geom_rect(
    data = pwc_age_rate_boundaries,
    aes(
      xmin = x_min,
      xmax = x_max,
      ymin = -Inf,
      ymax = Inf
    )
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$derivative_smooth,
    aes(
      x = age,
      ymin = ci_lower_adj,
      ymax = ci_upper_adj
    )
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = output_age_rate$predictions$derivative_smooth,
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = derivative_adj
    )
  ) +
  axes(
    x = "age",
    y = "hl_diff"
  ) +
  plot_theme() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 1, 0, unit = "pt"))


p <- align_plots(p0, p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, align = "hv")

p <- plot_grid(
  plot_grid(
    plot_grid(
      p[[1]],
      NULL,
      p[[2]],
      NULL,
      p[[3]],
      labels = c("A", "", "B", "", "C"),
      nrow = 1,
      rel_widths = c(1, -0.01, 1, -0.01, 1),
      label_x = 0.04,
      label_y = 1.02
    ),
    NULL,
    plot_grid(
      p[[4]],
      NULL,
      p[[5]],
      NULL,
      p[[6]],
      labels = c("D", "", "E", "", "F"),
      nrow = 1,
      rel_widths = c(1, -0.01, 1, -0.01, 1),
      label_x = 0.04,
      label_y = 1.02
    ),
    ncol = 1,
    rel_heights = c(1, -0.15, 1)
  ),
  NULL,
  plot_grid(
    NULL,
    p[[7]],
    NULL,
    ncol = 1,
    rel_heights = c(0.23, 0.54, 0.23),
    labels = c("", "G", ""),
    label_x = 0.04,
    label_y = 1.1
  ),
  ncol = 3,
  rel_widths = c(3, 0.1, 1.005)
)

sameplot(
  p,
  file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "age_rate_panel.png"),
  width = params$double_column,
  height = 3,
  res = 600,
  save = params$save_figures
)

8.2.2.2 Additional plots

8.2.2.2.1 Simplified Rate of Aging
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  p <- align_plots(p0, p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, align = "hv")

  p <- plot_grid(
    plot_grid(
      p[[1]],
      NULL,
      p[[2]],
      NULL,
      p[[3]],
      labels = c("A", "", "B", "", "C"),
      nrow = 1,
      rel_widths = c(1, -0.01, 1, -0.01, 1),
      label_x = 0.04,
      label_y = 1.02
    ),
    NULL,
    plot_grid(
      p[[4]],
      NULL,
      p[[5]],
      NULL,
      p[[6]],
      labels = c("D", "", "E", "", "F"),
      nrow = 1,
      rel_widths = c(1, -0.01, 1, -0.01, 1),
      label_x = 0.04,
      label_y = 1.02
    ),
    ncol = 1,
    rel_heights = c(1, -0.15, 1)
  )

  sameplot(
    p,
    file = here(
      plot_config("figure_dir"),
      "Extras",
      "simplified_age_rate_panel.png"
    ),
    width = params$double_column * 1.8,
    height = 3 * 1.8,
    scaling = 1.8,
    res = 600,
    save = params$save_figures
  )
}
8.2.2.2.2 Blank rate of aging
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  p0 <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = df,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
      )
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(1, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt"),
      axis.title.x = element_blank()
    )

  p1 <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = df,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
      )
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(1, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt"),
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      axis.title.x = element_blank()
    )

  p2 <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = df,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
      )
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 0, 0, unit = "pt"),
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      axis.title.x = element_blank()
    )

  p3 <- ggplot() +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$linear_derivative,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = derivative
      ),
      alpha = 0
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl_rate"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt"))

  p4 <- ggplot() +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$quadratic_derivative,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = derivative
      ),
      alpha = 0
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl_rate"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt"),
      axis.title.y = element_blank()
    )

  p5 <- ggplot() +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$derivative_smooth,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = derivative
      ),
      alpha = 0
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl_rate"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(0, 1, 0, 0, unit = "pt"),
      axis.title.y = element_blank()
    )

  p <- align_plots(p0, p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, align = "hv")

  p <- plot_grid(
    plot_grid(
      p[[1]],
      NULL,
      p[[2]],
      NULL,
      p[[3]],
      labels = c("A", "", "B", "", "C"),
      nrow = 1,
      rel_widths = c(1, -0.01, 1, -0.01, 1),
      label_x = 0.04,
      label_y = 1.02
    ),
    NULL,
    plot_grid(
      p[[4]],
      NULL,
      p[[5]],
      NULL,
      p[[6]],
      labels = c("D", "", "E", "", "F"),
      nrow = 1,
      rel_widths = c(1, -0.01, 1, -0.01, 1),
      label_x = 0.04,
      label_y = 1.02
    ),
    ncol = 1,
    rel_heights = c(1, -0.15, 1)
  )

  sameplot(
    p,
    file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "Extras", "blank_age_rate_panel.png"),
    width = params$double_column * 1.8,
    height = 3 * 1.8,
    scaling = 1.8,
    res = 600,
    save = params$save_figures
  )
}
8.2.2.2.3 Rate of aging gender
8.2.2.2.3.1 Calculate gender GAM
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  gam_gender <- bam(
    as.formula(
      paste0(
        params$hearing_var,
        "~ s(age, k = 10) + s(gender, age, bs = 'sz', k = 10)"
      )
    ),
    data = df,
    method = "REML",
    family = gaussian()
  )

  gender_predictions <- as.data.frame(
    confint(
      emmeans(
        gam_gender,
        as.formula(
          paste("~ age + gender")
        ),
        at = setNames(
          list(
            seq(
              min(df[["age"]]),
              max(df[["age"]]),
              by = 1
            )
          ),
          "age"
        ),
        type = "response",
        data = df
      ),
      adjust = "mvt"
    )
  ) %>%
    rename(
      "estimate" = emmean,
      "ci_lower" = lower.CL,
      "ci_upper" = upper.CL
    )

  gender_predictions_der <- data.frame(
    confint(
      emtrends(
        gam_gender,
        as.formula(
          paste("~ age + gender")
        ),
        var = "age",
        at = setNames(
          list(
            seq(
              min(
                df[["age"]]
              ),
              max(df[["age"]]),
              by = 1
            )
          ),
          "age"
        ),
        data = df
      ),
      adjust = "mvt"
    )
  ) %>%
    rename(
      "derivative" = !!paste0("age", ".trend"),
      "ci_lower" = lower.CL,
      "ci_upper" = upper.CL
    )
}
8.2.2.2.4 Plot rate of aging gender
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  p0 <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = df,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
      )
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$linear,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = estimate
      )
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$linear,
      aes(
        x = age,
        ymin = ci_lower,
        ymax = ci_upper
      )
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      axis.title.x = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt")
    )

  p1 <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = df,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
      )
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$quadratic,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = estimate
      )
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$quadratic,
      aes(
        x = age,
        ymin = ci_lower,
        ymax = ci_upper
      )
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      axis.title.x = element_blank(),
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt")
    )

  p2 <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = df,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
      )
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = gender_predictions,
      aes(
        x = age,
        ymin = ci_lower,
        ymax = ci_upper,
        fill = gender
      ),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = gender_predictions,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = estimate,
        color = gender
      ),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl"
    ) +
    color_scales("gender") +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      axis.title.x = element_blank(),
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 0, 0, unit = "pt")
    )

  p3 <- ggplot() +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$linear_derivative,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = derivative
      )
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$linear_derivative,
      aes(
        x = age,
        ymin = ci_lower,
        ymax = ci_upper
      )
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl_rate"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt"))

  p4 <- ggplot() +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$quadratic_derivative,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = derivative
      )
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$quadratic_derivative,
      aes(
        x = age,
        ymin = ci_lower,
        ymax = ci_upper
      )
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl_rate"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 1, unit = "pt")
    )

  p5 <- ggplot() +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = gender_predictions_der,
      aes(
        x = age,
        ymin = ci_lower,
        ymax = ci_upper,
        fill = gender
      ),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = gender_predictions_der,
      aes(x = age, y = derivative, color = gender),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl_rate"
    ) +
    color_scales("gender") +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(0, 1, 0, 0, unit = "pt")
    )

  p <- align_plots(p0, p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, align = "hv")

  p <- plot_grid(
    plot_grid(
      p[[1]],
      NULL,
      p[[2]],
      NULL,
      p[[3]],
      labels = c("A", "", "B", "", "C"),
      nrow = 1,
      rel_widths = c(1, -0.01, 1, -0.01, 1),
      label_x = 0.04,
      label_y = 1.02
    ),
    NULL,
    plot_grid(
      p[[4]],
      NULL,
      p[[5]],
      NULL,
      p[[6]],
      labels = c("D", "", "E", "", "F"),
      nrow = 1,
      rel_widths = c(1, -0.01, 1, -0.01, 1),
      label_x = 0.04,
      label_y = 1.02
    ),
    ncol = 1,
    rel_heights = c(1, -0.15, 1)
  )

  sameplot(
    p,
    file = here(
      plot_config("figure_dir"),
      "Extras",
      "simplified_age_rate_panel_gender.png"
    ),
    width = params$double_column * 1.8,
    height = 3 * 1.8,
    scaling = 1.8,
    res = 600,
    save = params$save_figures
  )
}
8.2.2.2.5 Plot aging means with and without confounders
8.2.2.2.5.1 Plot aging mean without confounders
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  p0 <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = df,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
      )
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = output_age_rate$predictions$smooth,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = estimate
      ),
      color = "grey80"
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = df %>%
        group_by(age) %>%
        summarize(
          hearing_var = mean(.data[[params$hearing_var]]),
          .groups = "drop"
        ),
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = hearing_var
      )
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl"
    ) +
    plot_theme()

  sameplot(
    p0,
    file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "Extras", "age_rate_mean.png"),
    width = params$single_column,
    height = 3,
    res = 600,
    save = params$save_figures
  )
}
8.2.2.2.5.2 Plot aging mean per gender
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  p0 <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = df,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
      )
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = gender_predictions,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = estimate,
        color = gender
      ),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = df %>%
        group_by(age, gender) %>%
        summarize(
          hearing_var = mean(.data[[params$hearing_var]]),
          .groups = "drop"
        ),
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = hearing_var,
        color = gender
      ),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl"
    ) +
    color_scales("gender") +
    plot_theme()

  sameplot(
    p0,
    file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "Extras", "age_rate_mean.png"),
    width = params$single_column,
    height = 3,
    res = 600,
    save = params$save_figures
  )
}
8.2.2.2.5.3 Plot aging mean per race
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  p0 <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = df,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = .data[[params$hearing_var]]
      )
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = df %>%
        group_by(age, race) %>%
        summarize(
          hearing_var = mean(.data[[params$hearing_var]]),
          .groups = "drop"
        ),
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = hearing_var,
        color = race
      )
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age",
      y = "hl"
    ) +
    color_scales("race") +
    plot_theme()

  sameplot(
    p0,
    file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "Extras", "age_rate_mean_race.png"),
    width = params$single_column,
    height = 3,
    res = 600,
    save = TRUE
  )
}

8.2.3 Bioage prediction panel

8.2.3.1 Bioage MSE plot

update_geom_defaults("point", set_geom_theme("mse_point"))
update_geom_defaults("line", set_geom_theme("mse_line"))

p <- df_summary_bioage %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age_group,
      y = mean_delta_mse
    )
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(
      fill = predictors,
      group = predictors,
      ymin = ci_low_delta_mse,
      ymax = ci_high_delta_mse
    ),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_line(
    aes(
      color = predictors,
      group = predictors
    ),
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      color = predictors
    ),
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = . %>%
      filter(p_adj != "ns"),
    aes(
      x = age_group,
      y = ci_high_delta_mse + 0.25,
      label = signif,
      color = predictors
    ),
    size = plot_config("signif_size") / (14 / 5)
  ) +
  axes(
    x = "age_group",
    y = "dmse_bioage"
  ) +
  color_scales("bioage") +
  plot_theme()

sameplot(
  p,
  file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "bioage_panel.png"),
  width = params$single_column,
  height = 2.15,
  res = 600,
  save = params$save_figures
)

8.2.3.2 Bioage RMSE plot (paper)

p <- df_summary_bioage %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age_group, y = delta_rmse
    )
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(
      fill = predictors,
      group = predictors,
      ymin = ci_low_delta_rmse,
      ymax = ci_high_delta_rmse
    ),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_line(
    aes(color = predictors, group = predictors),
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(color = predictors),
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = . %>%
      filter(p_adj != "ns"),
    aes(
      x = age_group,
      y = ci_high_delta_rmse + 0.01,
      label = signif,
      color = predictors
    ),
    size = plot_config("signif_size") / (14 / 5)
  ) +
  axes(
    x = "age_group",
    y = "drmse_bioage"
  ) +
  color_scales("bioage") +
  plot_theme()

sameplot(
  p,
  file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "bioage_panel.png"),
  width = params$single_column,
  height = 2.15,
  res = 600,
  save = params$save_figures
)

8.2.3.3 Additional Plots

8.2.3.3.1 Plot PhenoAge Only
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  p <- df_summary_bioage %>%
    filter(predictors %in% c("age", "age, phenoage")) %>%
    ggplot(
      aes(
        x = age_group,
        y = mean_delta_mse
      )
    ) +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      aes(
        fill = predictors,
        group = predictors,
        ymin = ci_low_delta_mse,
        ymax = ci_high_delta_mse
      ),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_line(
      aes(
        color = predictors,
        group = predictors
      ),
      show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    geom_point(
      aes(
        color = predictors
      ),
      show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = . %>%
        filter(p_adj != "ns"),
      aes(
        x = age_group,
        y = ci_high_delta_mse + 0.25,
        label = signif,
        color = predictors
      ),
      manual = TRUE,
      size = plot_config("signif_size") / (14 / 5)
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age_group",
      y = "dmse_bioage"
    ) +
    color_scales("phenoage") +
    plot_theme()

  sameplot(
    p,
    file = here(
      plot_config("figure_dir"),
      "Extras",
      "phenoage_only_bioage_panel.png"
    ),
    width = params$single_column * 1.8,
    height = 2.15 * 1.8,
    scaling = 1.8,
    res = 600,
    save = params$save_figures
  )
}
8.2.3.3.2 Advance bioage plot
p <- df %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(
    aes(
      x = phenoage_advance,
      y = age_group
    )
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = "Decelerated Aging",
    hjust = 2.7,
    vjust = 1,
    size = plot_config("text_size_primary") / (14 / 5),
    color = "darkgreen"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = "Accelerated Aging",
    hjust = 1,
    vjust = 1,
    size = plot_config("text_size_primary") / (14 / 5),
    color = "darkred"
  ) +
  axes(
    x = "age_accel",
    y = "age_group"
  ) +
  color_scales("bioage") +
  plot_theme()

sameplot(
  p,
  file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "Extras", "age_acceleration.png"),
  width = params$single_column,
  height = 2.15,
  res = 600,
  save = params$save_figures
)
## Picking joint bandwidth of 1.04
## Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's colour values.
## Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.

8.2.3.3.3 Confounders predictive capacity plot
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  p <- df_summary_confounders %>%
    ggplot(
      aes(
        x = age_group,
        y = mean_delta_mse
      )
    ) +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      aes(
        fill = predictors,
        group = predictors,
        ymin = ci_low_delta_mse,
        ymax = ci_high_delta_mse
      ),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_line(
      aes(
        color = predictors,
        group = predictors
      ),
      show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    geom_point(
      aes(
        color = predictors
      ),
      show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = . %>%
        filter(p_adj != "ns"),
      aes(
        x = age_group,
        y = ci_high_delta_mse,
        label = signif,
        color = predictors
      ),
      manual = TRUE,
      size = plot_config("signif_size") / (14 / 5)
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age_group",
      y = "dmse_conf"
    ) +
    color_scales("confounders") +
    plot_theme()

  sameplot(
    p,
    file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "confounder_panel.png"),
    width = params$single_column,
    height = 2.15,
    res = 600,
    save = params$save_figures
  )
}
8.2.3.3.4 Plot additional variance confounders
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  p <- df_summary_confounders %>%
    ggplot(
      aes(
        x = age_group,
        y = mean_additional_variance
      )
    ) +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      aes(
        fill = predictors,
        group = predictors,
        ymin = ci_low_additional_variance,
        ymax = ci_high_additional_variance
      ),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_line(
      aes(
        color = predictors,
        group = predictors
      ),
      show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    geom_point(
      aes(
        color = predictors
      ),
      show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = . %>%
        filter(p_adj != "ns"),
      aes(
        x = age_group,
        y = ci_high_additional_variance,
        label = signif,
        color = predictors
      ),
      manual = TRUE,
      size = plot_config("signif_size") / (14 / 5)
    ) +
    axes(
      x = "age_group",
      y = "add_pred_conf"
    ) +
    color_scales("confounders") +
    plot_theme()

  sameplot(
    p,
    file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "confounder_panel_var.png"),
    width = params$single_column,
    height = 2.15,
    res = 600,
    save = params$save_figures
  )
}
8.2.3.3.5 Biomarker plots
8.2.3.3.5.1 Plot Delta mse Biomarkers (only relevant ones)
8.2.3.3.5.2 Plot biomarkers together vs biomarkers + phenoage
8.2.3.3.5.3 Composite plot
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  p <- ggarrange(
    p1,
    NULL,
    p2,
    nrow = 1,
    widths = c(0.6, 0, 1),
    labels = c("A", "", "B"),
    label.x = 0.02,
    label.y = 1.01,
    align = "v"
  )

  sameplot(
    p,
    file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "biomarkers_fig_panel.png"),
    width = params$double_column,
    height = 3,
    res = 600,
    save = params$save_figures
  )
}
8.2.3.3.6 Biomarkers supplemental plots
if (isFALSE(params$telolength)) {
  update_geom_defaults("linerange", set_geom_theme("biomarker_linerange"))
  update_geom_defaults("point", set_geom_theme("biomarker_point"))

  p <- df_summary_biomarkers_overall %>%
    filter(!(
      predictors %in% c(
        "age",
        "age, phenoage",
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL",
        "age, crp, hba1c, albumin_gL, phenoage"
      )
    )) %>%
    mutate(predictors = factor(
      predictors,
      levels = c(
        "age, sbp",
        "age, bun",
        "age, mcv",
        "age, lymph",
        "age, rdw",
        "age, totchol",
        "age, creat",
        "age, uap",
        "age, glucose",
        "age, wbc",
        "age, alp",
        "age, albumin_gL",
        "age, hba1c",
        "age, crp"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "SBP",
        "BUN",
        "MCV",
        "Lymphs",
        "RDW",
        "TC",
        "Creat",
        "UA",
        "Glc",
        "WBC",
        "ALP",
        "ALB",
        "HbA1c",
        "CRP"
      )
    )) %>%
    ggplot(
      aes(
        x = predictors,
        y = mean_delta_mse
      )
    ) +
    geom_point(
      aes(
        color = predictors,
        fill = predictors
      ),
      position = position_dodge(0.8),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_linerange(
      aes(
        color = predictors,
        ymin = ci_low_delta_mse,
        ymax = ci_high_delta_mse
      ),
      position = position_dodge(0.8),
      lineend = "round",
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = . %>%
        filter(p_adj != "ns"),
      aes(
        x = predictors,
        y = ci_high_delta_mse + 0.25,
        label = signif,
        color = predictors
      ),
      size = plot_config("signif_size") / (14 / 5),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    axes(
      y = "dmse_biomark"
    ) +
    plot_theme() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.25),
      axis.text.x = element_markdown(
        "sans",
        size = 8,
        angle = 45,
        hjust = 1,
        vjust = 1
      ),
      axis.title.x = element_blank()
    )

  sameplot(
    p,
    file = here(plot_config("figure_dir"), "biomarkers_panel.png"),
    width = params$single_column,
    height = 2.15,
    res = 600,
    save = params$save_figures
  )
}
